title: Stellar Private Payments
description: Stellar Private Payments is a Nethermind-built proof-of-concept shielded pool on the Stellar network (Soroban smart contracts) that lets users deposit, privately transfer, and withdraw tokens using Groth16 zero-knowledge proofs. Funds are held as UTXO-style note commitments in an on-chain Merkle tree and spent via nullifiers, so amounts and note contents stay hidden. An Association Set Provider (ASP) layer enforces compliance: the transaction circuit proves each spent input's key belongs to an admin-curated membership Merkle tree and is absent from an exclusion Sparse Merkle Tree. It is an unaudited testnet PoC.

properties.name: Anonymity set size
properties.notes: The commitment Merkle tree is fixed-depth with capacity 2^levels (the testnet pool is deployed with levels = 10, so 1024 leaves). As an explicitly-labeled testnet proof of concept, real usage is negligible, so no meaningful anonymity set has accrued.

properties.name: Confidentiality
properties.notes: Each note is a Poseidon2 commitment hash(amount, publicKey, blinding), so amounts are hidden, and note ciphertext is encrypted to the recipient's X25519 key. A balance is just the sum of a user's unspent notes and is never stored in the clear. Confidentiality holds inside the pool. At the boundary, the public amount of a deposit or withdrawal (ext_amount) is visible on-chain.

properties.name: Anonymity
properties.notes: The core guarantee is unlinkability: a withdrawal proves note ownership via a nullifier without revealing which deposit it came from, breaking the on-chain link between the deposit and withdrawal addresses. In-pool transfers move notes between pseudonymous note keys, hiding both sender and receiver. Boundary interactions stay public. Deposits and withdrawals expose a Stellar address and amount, and the optional register() call links an address to its note key.

properties.name: Asset privacy
properties.notes: Each pool instance is single-asset (the testnet pool is deployed for native XLM), and the token a pool handles is fixed at construction and public, so the asset being transferred is always known.

properties.name: Plausible deniability
properties.notes: Transacting calls the pool contract directly, which is a distinct and observable Soroban invocation. It is not indistinguishable from an ordinary Stellar payment, so usage of the privacy protocol is detectable.

properties.name: Time-to-finality
properties.notes: SPP is an application on Stellar and does not define its own finality. Settlement finality is inherited from the underlying Stellar network, whose consensus protocol finalizes a ledger on close with no reorgs.

properties.name: Number of secrets
properties.notes: The user's only secret is their Stellar wallet (Freighter) seed. The BN254 note-identity keypair and the X25519 encryption keypair are both derived deterministically from wallet signatures over fixed messages (SPENDING_KEY_MESSAGE and ENCRYPTION_MESSAGE), so only the wallet seed needs to be backed up.

properties.name: Deposit time
properties.notes: There is no protocol-enforced waiting period for a deposit. The user generates a proof locally and submits a single transact() call, and the resulting note is spendable once the Stellar transaction settles. The only latency is local proof generation, which is not measured here.

properties.name: Withdraw time
properties.notes: There is no protocol-enforced waiting period or vetting window for a withdrawal. The user generates a proof locally and submits a single transact() call that releases funds to the recipient once the Stellar transaction settles. The only latency is local proof generation, which is not measured here.

properties.name: Censorship resistance
properties.notes: A valid transfer or withdrawal additionally requires proving that the spender's key is in the admin-managed ASP membership Merkle tree and absent from the exclusion Sparse Merkle Tree. The ASP admin can withhold membership or add an exclusion to block an otherwise-valid spend, so the protocol can censor users at the application layer regardless of Stellar's permissionless access.

properties.name: External network dependence
properties.notes: Beyond the Stellar network itself, every transact() call requires a proof generated against the current roots of the admin-run ASP membership and non-membership contracts, which the pool re-checks on-chain. If the admin does not include a user's key, excludes it, or stops maintaining the ASP, that user's deposits, transfers, and withdrawals are blocked, so the protocol depends on a permissioned compliance authority to authorize interactions.

properties.name: Escape hatch
properties.notes: Withdrawing requires satisfying the admin-controlled ASP policy proof, so there is no admin-independent path to recover funds if membership is revoked or an exclusion is added. Separately, Stellar RPC retains contract events for only ~7 days, so a user who loses local state after that window may be unable to reconstruct their notes to spend them.

properties.name: Upgradeability
properties.notes: This reflects the current testnet proof-of-concept stage and is a work in progress. Today a single admin address (the testnet deployer account) can rotate the admin and repoint the ASP membership and non-membership contracts, which effectively changes the enforced policy. The deployed pool exposes no WASM upgrade entrypoint and the verifier is fixed at construction. The governance and upgrade model is expected to change before any mainnet deployment.
properties.needsResearchReview: Admin is a single externally-owned account in the current testnet deployment, not a finalized production governance setup. Classification reflects the PoC stage.

properties.name: Client-side proving
properties.notes: Groth16 proofs are generated entirely in the browser by a dedicated WASM Prover Web Worker that loads proving artifacts in memory. There is no external proving service.

properties.name: Third-party inspectability
properties.notes: Note contents are encrypted to the recipient's X25519 key, derived from the owner's wallet, so only the owner can decrypt and read their balances and history. Optional pool-admin view keys (view-only, with or without note traceability) are currently being developed as part of the project's phase 2 work and are not present in the current implementation.

properties.name: Implementation maturity
properties.notes: The contracts are deployed to the Stellar testnet with a live demo app, and the project is presented explicitly as an unaudited proof of concept. The Groth16 trusted setup was only a small internal ceremony, and the team states a larger, more decentralized ceremony would be needed before any mainnet deployment. Documented limitations include a single 2-in/2-out circuit and reliance on a ~7-day RPC event-retention window. It is not on mainnet.
properties.needsResearchReview: Value reflects an unaudited testnet PoC; the testnet deployment date is not tied to a dated primary source.

properties.name: Post-quantum secure
properties.notes: Security rests on BN254 pairing-based Groth16 proofs and Poseidon2 hashing. The underlying pairing and discrete-log assumptions are broken by Shor's algorithm on a sufficiently large quantum computer.

properties.name: Layer of enforcement
properties.notes: Enforcement is performed by the Association Set Provider (ASP) at the protocol level rather than by the underlying asset or a removable frontend. The transaction circuit requires a membership proof (the user's key is in the ASP allow-list) and a non-membership proof (the key is absent from the exclusion block-list), and the pool contract rejects any transaction whose ASP roots do not match the current on-chain roots.

properties.name: Enforcement entities
properties.notes: An ASP admin maintains the approved-key membership tree and the exclusion Sparse Merkle Tree. Leaf insertion is admin-gated by default (AdminInsertOnly = true) and the admin can toggle it to permissionless.
properties.needsResearchReview: Admin is a single externally-owned testnet account; production governance composition is undefined.

properties.name: Type of compliance
properties.notes: An Association Set Provider model: the transaction circuit proves, for each spent input, that the input's public key IS present in an approved membership Merkle tree AND is NOT present in an exclusion Sparse Merkle Tree. The same ZK primitive is used for both the allow and block sets.

properties.name: Point of enforcement
properties.notes: The ASP policy is keyed on the user's public key, and the circuit forces every interaction with the pool to prove that key is currently in the membership allow-list and absent from the exclusion block-list. Because the check binds the spender's key rather than a specific deposit, it applies uniformly to deposits, in-pool transfers, and withdrawals.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing entity
properties.notes: Compliance is handled by the ASP rather than by sharing transaction data, and there is no third-party selective-disclosure mechanism live today. Owners can decrypt and view their own notes using keys derived from their wallet (self-view). Broader selective disclosure, where a user binds a note to a context and chooses how much to reveal, is currently being developed as part of the project's phase 2 work.

properties.name: Selective disclosure: viewing control
properties.notes: There is no viewing-key system live in the protocol today, so there are no viewing permissions to define or update. The only current visibility is self-decryption of a user's own notes. Programmable viewing controls, including pool-admin view keys, are currently being developed as part of the project's phase 2 work.

properties.name: Cryptographic verifiability
properties.notes: Transaction validity (input ownership, nullifier correctness, Merkle membership of spent commitments, balance conservation, and the ASP membership/non-membership policy) is guaranteed by a Groth16 zk-SNARK over BN254 that is verified on-chain by the verifier contract before any state change.

properties.name: Open source
properties.notes: The source code is public on GitHub under the Apache License 2.0, with circuits/build.rs licensed separately under LGPLv3.

properties.name: Private State Scalability
properties.notes: The spent-nullifier set kept in contract storage grows without bound as usage increases. The commitment Merkle tree is append-only with fixed capacity (2^levels), so it stops accepting deposits once full rather than pruning.

properties.name: Client-side indexing
properties.notes: The client runs an indexer that continuously polls Stellar RPC for commitment events and trial-decrypts them to find notes addressed to the user. Discovery is constrained by Stellar RPC's ~7-day event-retention window.

properties.name: Private state model
properties.notes: Funds are notes (commitments) that are consumed via nullifiers and recreated as new commitments, forming a UTXO set rather than per-account balances. A spend nullifies input notes and creates up to two output notes (2-in/2-out circuit).

properties.name: Private Data Storage
properties.notes: The commitment Merkle tree and the spent-nullifier set live in the pool's Soroban contract storage. Encrypted note ciphertexts are emitted as contract events and cached client-side in OPFS-backed SQLite.

properties.name: Access to DeFi
properties.notes: It is an isolated single-asset privacy pool with no composability into other Stellar/Soroban DeFi applications. Shielded funds can only be deposited, transferred privately, and withdrawn.

properties.name: Programmability / Generality
properties.notes: The system supports only deposit, private transfer, and withdraw of a single token via one fixed 2-input/2-output circuit, with no general private smart-contract logic. The README notes that multi-circuit support is only a possible future extension.
